pr.title: Fix Fighter user_data accesses using `GET_FIGHTER` macro
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: Standardized many Fighter pointer fetches to use GET_FIGHTER instead of direct user_data access or getFighter/HSD_GObjGetUserData variants, and introduced union Struct2070 so the Fighter fields at 0x2070 can be accessed both as a packed s32 and as named bitfields. The main matching-relevant change replaced a volatile local stack workaround in Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC with a local union copy, preserving access to x2070_int while allowing x2071/x2072 bitfield members to live under fp->x2070. Review accepted the union as useful but left a todo to rename/investigate broader use.
what_changed: Changed direct Fighter user data retrievals such as fighter_gobj->user_data and HSD_GObjGetUserData(fighter_gobj) to GET_FIGHTER(fighter_gobj) in multiple fighter.c routines.
Replaced getFighter(otherObj) with GET_FIGHTER(otherObj) in Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC.
Added union Struct2070 in src/melee/ft/types.h with bitfield members x2071_b0_3, x2071_b4 through x2071_b7, x2072_b0_3, x2072_b4 through x2072_b7, x2073, and whole-word x2070_int.
Changed Fighter's previous flat 0x2070/x2071/x2072/x2073 members into a single field, union Struct2070 x2070, at offset 0x2070.
Updated character code to access these packed flags through fp->x2070, for example fp->x2070.x2071_b6, fp->x2070.x2072_b4, fp->x2070.x2071_b0_3, and fp->x2070.x2073.

In Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC, replaced a volatile s32 local used to keep stack/order behavior with a local union Struct2070 x2070 assigned from fp->x2070, then passed x2070.x2070_int to func_80037C60.

Removed or inlined several small helper functions/macros in fighter.c, including getHSDJObj, pickValue, CLEAR_STICKS, Fighter_UnkApplyTransformation_8006C0F0_Inner1, and Fighter_8006C80C_Inline_1, where the diff shows equivalent direct expressions or GET_JOBJ usage.

Added comments/todos around matching-sensitive spots, including unused stack in Fighter_CallAcessoryCallbacks_8006C624, an inline/stack note in Fighter_UnkApplyTransformation_8006C0F0, and a question near recursive hitlag handling.
Left Fighter_Unload_8006DABC using its direct void* user_data argument, with a remark that GET_FIGHTER is not used because the function appears to receive Fighter user data directly.
smart_moves: Used a union to model the same 0x2070 region both as bitfields and as a full s32, avoiding a brittle volatile variable workaround while retaining the whole-word value needed by func_80037C60.
Centralized Fighter pointer recovery through GET_FIGHTER, making future code less dependent on whether HSD_GObj user data is accessed directly, through a helper, or through HSD_GObjGetUserData.
Kept bitfield names offset-derived and mechanically searchable, which is appropriate while semantics are still unknown.
Did not force GET_FIGHTER onto Fighter_Unload_8006DABC where the parameter is already raw user_data; the added remark documents the exception instead of hiding it.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack behavior with explicit local variables and MUST_MATCH-only unused stack where needed rather than over-abstracting.
decomp_lessons: For HSD_GObj routines that operate on fighters, prefer GET_FIGHTER(gobj) over direct gobj->user_data casts unless the function is explicitly passed raw user_data.
When a region of Fighter is accessed both as individual flags and as a packed integer, a union can replace volatile locals and make the intended aliasing explicit.
If a helper inline only exists to affect stack/codegen, consider whether a direct expression plus a documented todo is clearer and equally matchable.
Header struct changes can unblock broad source cleanup: once x2070 was represented as a union, character code could use consistent fp->x2070.<field> accesses.
Keep TODOs on provisional struct/union names and unknown flag meanings; reviewers accepted Struct2070 only with an explicit rename/investigate note.
Use comments to mark suspected inline/stack artifacts, but do not invent semantic function names unless evidence is strong.
naming_conventions: GET_FIGHTER is the preferred macro name for retrieving Fighter* from HSD_GObj*.
GET_JOBJ appears as the preferred macro for retrieving HSD_JObj* from an HSD_GObj* in the shown transformation code.
Unknown Fighter fields retained offset-based names such as x2070, x2071_b6, x2072_b4, and x2073.
Bitfield names used the pattern xOFFSET_bBIT or xOFFSET_bSTART_END, for example x2071_b0_3 and x2072_b4.
The union was named Struct2070 only provisionally; review added `/// @todo Rename this and its members; investigate using it elsewhere.`
Local Fighter pointers were normalized toward fp rather than longer register-derived names such as fighter_r31 in the shown cleanup.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a volatile s32 stack-order workaround with `union Struct2070 x2070;` followed by `x2070 = fp->x2070;`, then used `x2070.x2070_int` for the packed argument.
Used a union overlay to make bitfield and integer loads come from the same Fighter offset without unsafe ad hoc casts in user code.
Inlining the pickValue ternary directly preserved the expression at callsites to func_8006EBE8 instead of relying on a helper function.
Directly zeroed substick x/y fields instead of using the removed CLEAR_STICKS macro, likely to keep the generated stores straightforward.
Added MUST_MATCH-only unused stack storage in Fighter_CallAcessoryCallbacks_8006C624 to account for stack differences without affecting non-matching builds.
A transformation routine documents that an inline would usually imply the pattern, but the removed inline reserved more stack than the single allocation needed.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned extracting Struct2070 into its own type; agreed the union replacing a volatile variable made sense, but suggested it might be inlineable into Fighter.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested that if this pattern reflects HAL usage, more Fighter flag members may need conversion to similar union-based representations.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Proposed merging with a todo: `Rename this and its members; investigate using it elsewhere.`

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Agreed with merging using the todo.

follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `fighter_gobj->user_data` or `HSD_GObjGetUserData` uses in fighter-owned HSD_GObj paths that should become GET_FIGHTER.
Investigate whether other Fighter packed flag regions should be modeled as unions like Struct2070.
Rename Struct2070 and its members once the semantics of x2071/x2072/x2073 flags are known.
Check whether x2071_b6 shared use in Fox blaster, Mewtwo shadow ball, Samus, and Fighter_ActionStateChange reveals a semantic name.
Review MUST_MATCH unused stack comments in fighter.c to see whether later compiler/matching improvements removed the need for them.
